steps.name: judge_scores_joint
steps.content:
E=0.90 — Efficacy (Low vs High) is the most decisive determinant of 5-year overall outcome; higher efficacy directly lowers disease progression/mortality risk.

A=0.80 — Adherence (High vs Low) is highly decisive because even a highly efficacious drug fails if not taken; substantial impact on real-world effectiveness over 5 years.

S=0.60 — Safety (High vs Low) moderately decisive: safety differences can drive morbidity, treatment discontinuation and mortality, but typically less directly predictive of disease-specific benefit than efficacy/adherence.

D=0.50 — Durability (Low vs High) moderately/modest decisive: longer durability reduces relapse/retreatment over 5 years, but its impact is less than efficacy/adherence and comparable to safety.
